steps.title: Use the probability formula
steps.content:
The probability of selecting a blue marble is:
$$\frac{\text{number of blue marbles}}{\text{total number of marbles}}$$

steps.title: Read the table
steps.content:
The table shows $12$ blue marbles and $40$ marbles total, so the probability is:
$$\frac{12}{40}$$

steps.title: Check the choices
steps.content: Choice B — $19 / 40$ uses the green marbles, and Choice C — $21 / 40$ uses red plus blue. The event asked for blue only, so use the blue count over the total.
